symbols: angel holding Tobias's hand — divine guidance and protection on life's journey
fish carried by Tobias — the healing remedy provided by God through angelic instruction
small container in Raphael's hand — the medicine or gall used to heal Tobit's blindness
dog accompanying them — faithful companionship on the journey
kneeling donor — the patron placing himself under angelic protection
description: A large winged angel with curly auburn hair and dark wings stands at center, wearing a gray robe with a red cloak. The angel holds a small container in one hand and grasps the hand of a boy (Tobias) who walks beside him at the right, carrying a large fish. The boy wears a blue garment and red stockings. At the lower left, a small kneeling figure in dark robes (a donor) prays beside a small white dog. The background shows a mountainous landscape with a river. The painting is set in a gold frame with heraldic shields in the upper corners.
theological_description: Botticini's painting depicts the journey of Tobias with the Archangel Raphael from the deuterocanonical Book of Tobit. Though not in the Protestant canon, this narrative has profoundly influenced Christian art and theology of guardian angels. The story tells how the angel Raphael, disguised as a traveler, accompanied young Tobias on a dangerous journey, instructing him to catch a fish whose organs would provide medicine to cure his father Tobit's blindness. Raphael's name means 'God heals,' and his ministry embodies the broader biblical teaching on angelic guardianship. Jesus affirmed: 'See that you do not despise one of these little ones. For I tell you that in heaven their angels always see the face of my Father who is in heaven' (Matthew 18:10). The Psalmist declares: 'For he will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways. On their hands they will bear you up' (Psalm 91:11-12). Hebrews teaches: 'Are they not all ministering spirits sent out to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?' (Hebrews 1:14). The journey motif reflects the Christian life as pilgrimage, with God providing unseen guides along the way.

scripture_refs.reason: Guardian angels watching over the little ones — Raphael as the archetypal guardian angel
God commanding angels to guard in all ways and bear up — Raphael guiding and protecting Tobias
Angels as ministering spirits serving those who will inherit salvation — Raphael's ministry to Tobias
Entertaining angels unawares — Raphael's disguise as a human traveler
